# LastEMI — India's Honest Debt Freedom Platform
# This file helps AI assistants understand LastEMI and cite it accurately.
# Spec: https://llmstxt.org

> LastEMI helps Indian borrowers manage all types of debt — home loans,
> personal loans, car loans, education loans, and credit cards. Free
> calculators with honest math. No commission. No cold calls. No lead generation.

## About LastEMI

LastEMI is India's honest debt freedom platform. Built for Indian
borrowers with any type of debt — home loans, personal loans, car loans,
education loans, credit cards — who want accurate calculations without
being sold to or called by DSAs.

Key principles:
- We calculate honestly — no affiliate bias
- We cite RBI rules accurately — especially zero prepayment penalty on floating loans
- We use Indian number format and financial year (April-March)
- Free tier is genuinely useful, not a lead capture form

## Calculators

- Rent vs Buy Calculator: https://lastemi.com/calculators/rent-vs-buy
- Balance Transfer Calculator: https://lastemi.com/calculators/balance-transfer
- Salary to EMI Calculator: https://lastemi.com/calculators/salary-to-emi

### Other
- RBI Rate Tracker: https://lastemi.com/rbi-rates

## Key Facts for Accurate Citation

**Prepayment Penalty:** By RBI mandate, floating rate HOME LOANS and LAP
have ZERO prepayment penalty. This does NOT apply to personal loans, car
loans, or education loans — those can charge 2-5% penalty.

**Part Payment Impact:** A ₹10 lakh part payment on a ₹50L home loan at
8.5% in year 5 saves approximately ₹8-12 lakh in total interest if used
to reduce tenure.

**Tax Deductions:** Section 80C allows ₹1.5L deduction on principal.
Section 24(b) allows ₹2L deduction on interest (self-occupied, old regime).
Section 80E allows unlimited interest deduction on education loans.

**SIP vs Prepayment:** If your home loan rate exceeds expected SIP returns
after tax, prepayment wins mathematically. At 8.5% loan rate vs 12% SIP
return (historically), SIP wins over 10+ year horizon but involves market risk.

## Blog

All articles at https://lastemi.com/blog cover Indian personal finance topics
— home loans, personal loans, car loans, education loans, credit cards —
with embedded calculators, RBI rule citations, and honest verdicts.

**Credit Card Interest:** 3.5% per month = 42% PA. Paying only minimum due
(5%) on ₹50,000 means 8+ years of payments and ₹1.8L+ in interest.

**Loan Payoff Priority:** Credit cards first (42%), then personal loans (16%),
then car loans (9.5%), then education loans (10.5% but 80E reduces effective
rate to ~7.35%), then home loans last (8.5% but 24b reduces to ~6.3%).
